message:
feat(brenner-loop): implement robustness score calculation

Adds RobustnessScore interface and computeRobustness function that
quantifies how well a hypothesis has survived testing:

- Weighted survival score based on discriminative power of tests
- Falsifiability score from hypothesis structure
- Specificity score from prediction precision
- Interpretations: robust, shaky, falsified, untested

Includes 40 unit tests covering all calculation paths.

Closes brenner_bot-an1n.4

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"brenner_bot-ggv","title":"Web: landing page + navigation","description":"# Purpose

The landing page is the first impression. It needs to:
1. Immediately communicate what Brenner Bot is
2. Show why the Brenner method matters
3. Provide clear navigation to key content
4. Showcase memorable Brenner quotes

# Key Sections

1. **Hero** - Title, one-liner, animated terminal or quote carousel
2. **What Is This** - Brief explanation with links to deeper content
3. **Feature Cards** - Corpus, Distillations, How It Works, Sessions
4. **Signature Quotes** - Rotating or scrollable Brenner quotes
5. **Navigation** - Top nav with corpus, distillations, method, sessions links

# Design Notes

- Hero should have visual movement (animated terminal showing Brenner quotes, or quote carousel)
- Feature cards with gradient hover effects
- Signature quotes in elegant quote blocks
- Responsive: stack on mobile, grid on desktop

# Depends On

- Design System Foundation (for tokens, fonts, shadcn setup)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:11:16.275776333-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:48.453216776-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T21:32:48.453216776-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: landing page has hero with animated gradient, feature cards, stats, quote section, lab mode card","labels":["landing","ux","web"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ggv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1qx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:12:25.323699818-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-ggv","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-5so.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-29T19:20:24.319423016-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-ggx","title":"Task: Unit tests for lib/agentMail.ts","description":"Write comprehensive unit tests for Agent Mail client.

## Functions to Test
- AgentMailClient class methods
- HTTP request formatting
- Response parsing
- Error handling

## Test Approach
Since we avoid mocks, options are:
1. Test against a real Agent Mail server (integration test)
2. Test request/response formatting in isolation
3. Use test fixtures for response parsing

## Test Cases
- Valid request construction
- Response parsing (success cases)
- Error response handling
- Connection failure handling
- Timeout handling
- Authentication header construction","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:10:36.35189013-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-29T22:59:22.620875881-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T22:59:22.620875881-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive unit tests for agentMail.ts (42 tests passing, 2 skipped for server availability).  {"id":"brenner_bot-gnj","title":"Feature: Search Infrastructure","description":"Build the core search infrastructure: index generation, storage, and query engine.

## Rationale

Before building UI, we need the search engine working. This is the foundation everything else depends on.

## Technical Decision: Client-Side Search

**Why client-side over server-side:**
- Corpus is small (~589KB text, ~68k words)
- Zero latency after initial load
- Works offline
- Simpler infrastructure (no search server)
- Fits in browser memory easily

**Library choice: FlexSearch**
- Fastest full-text search for JavaScript
- Supports partial matching and relevance scoring
- Small bundle size (~6KB gzipped core)
- Used by VS Code, Notion, others

Alternative: MiniSearch (smaller, good enough for our needs)

## Architecture

### Index Generation
- Run at BUILD TIME (not runtime)
- Parse all corpus documents
- Extract: title, content, section anchors, document type
- Output: JSON index file for client consumption

### Index Contents
For each searchable unit (section/paragraph):
```
{
  docId: string,
  docTitle: string,
  sectionId: string,
  sectionTitle: string,
  content: string,
  anchor: string (for linking)
}
```

### Query Engine
- Debounced search (150ms after typing stops)
- Return top N results with relevance scores
- Include context snippets around matches

## Deliverables

- Build-time index generation script
- Search index JSON file
- Client-side query engine wrapper
- Unit tests for search accuracy

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Index generated at build time
- [ ] Index includes all 7 corpus documents
- [ ] Search returns results in <50ms
- [ ] Relevance ranking works (exact matches higher)
- [ ] Index size < 150KB compressed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:09.654930258-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:06:29.781170126-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:06:29.781170126-05:00","close_reason":"All 4 subtasks closed. Build-time index generation via MiniSearch, 7 documents indexed, fast search (<50ms), relevance ranking implemented in computeScore function. Index size ~150KB.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-gnj","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-cxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T22:27:23.853744108-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-gson","title":"Task: Add Test Execution CLI Commands","description":"# Task: Add Test Execution CLI Commands

## Objective
CLI commands for managing tests, recording executions, and applying result bindings.

## Implementation Details

### Commands

#### `brenner test list`
```bash
brenner test list [--status=designed|completed|blocked] [--session=<id>]

# Output:
ID                | Status    | Description (truncated)           | Targets
------------------|-----------|-----------------------------------|--------
T-RS20251230-001  | completed | Lineage tracing with Cre-lox...   | H1, H2
T-RS20251230-002  | designed  | Single-cell ATAC-seq...           | H1, H3
T-RS20251231-001  | blocked   | ChIP-seq for H3K27me3...          | H3
```

#### `brenner test show <id>`
```bash
brenner test show T-RS20251230-001

# Output:
Test: T-RS20251230-001
Description: Lineage tracing with Cre-lox to track cell fate decisions

Status: completed
Designed in: RS-20251230
Designed by: PurpleMountain (claude-code)

Predictions:
  H-RS20251230-001: If epigenetically determined, daughter cells
                    maintain parental methylation patterns
  H-RS20251230-002: If transcriptionally determined, daughter cells
                    show variable expression patterns

Execution:
  Executed: 2025-12-31T10:00:00Z
  Result: Daughter cells showed variable methylation but stable expression
  Matched: (none)
  Violated: H-RS20251230-001, H-RS20251230-002
  Confidence: medium
  Notes: Both hypotheses were too narrow. Third alternative needed.
```

#### `brenner test execute <id>`
Interactive command for recording test execution:
```bash
brenner test execute T-RS20251230-002

# Interactive prompts:
? Enter the result/observation:
> Daughter cells showed variable methylation but stable expression

? For H-RS20251230-001 ("If epigenetically determined..."):
  ○ Matched (prediction correct)
  ● Violated (prediction incorrect)
  ○ Inconclusive

? For H-RS20251230-002 ("If transcriptionally determined..."):
  ○ Matched
  ● Violated
  ○ Inconclusive

? Confidence in interpretation:
  ○ High (clear, unambiguous result)
  ● Medium (reasonable interpretation)
  ○ Low (uncertain, needs more data)

? Any notes? (optional):
> Both hypotheses were too narrow. Need to consider chromatin accessibility.

✓ Recorded execution for T-RS20251230-002

Suggested state transitions:
  H-RS20251230-001: active → killed (violated prediction)
  H-RS20251230-002: active → killed (violated prediction)

? Apply these transitions?
  ● Yes, apply all
  ○ Review individually
  ○ No, skip for now

✓ Applied 2 transitions
```

#### `brenner test suggest-kills <id>`
```bash
brenner test suggest-kills T-RS20251230-002

# Output (for completed tests):
Based on execution results for T-RS20251230-002:

Suggested kills:
  H-RS20251230-001: Prediction violated (medium confidence)
    Predicted: daughter cells maintain parental methylation patterns
    Observed: variable methylation but stable expression
  
  H-RS20251230-002: Prediction violated (medium confidence)
    Predicted: variable expression patterns
    Observed: stable expression patterns

Run `brenner hypothesis kill <id> --test=T-RS20251230-002` to apply.
```

#### `brenner test bind <test-id> <hypothesis-id>`
Manual binding for edge cases:
```bash
brenner test bind T-RS20251230-002 H-RS20251230-001 --matched|--violated

# Output:
✓ Bound T-RS20251230-002 to H-RS20251230-001 (violated)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List tests with filtering
- [ ] Show test with predictions and execution
- [ ] Interactive execution recording
- [ ] Automatic transition suggestions
- [ ] Optional transition application
- [ ] Manual binding for edge cases
- [ ] Clear audit trail

## Dependencies
- Task: Define Test Record and Execution Schemas
- Task: Implement Test Storage Layer
- Task: Implement Prediction Binding Logic

## Effort Estimate
~350 lines of code, ~4 hours","notes":"Implemented `brenner test` CLI commands (list/show/execute/suggest-kills/bind) with `--apply` to auto-confirm/refute, plus CLI coverage in `brenner.test.ts`.

Follow-up: interactive prompt UX for `brenner test execute` is tracked in brenner_bot-77nk.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownSnow","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:46:57.885554431-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-31T03:56:04.344054765-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T03:55:41.087380267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented  CLI commands (list/show/execute/suggest-kills/bind) with  + added CLI tests; interactive execute UX tracked in brenner_bot-77nk.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-gson","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-m6yw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.096249245-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-gson","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-x0o2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.316407371-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-gson","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-e4sb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.347171218-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-gson","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-evxc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-30T19:49:02.379991436-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-gson","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-jcuz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-30T22:41:33.879459117-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"brenner_bot-gsp","title":"Design: JargonTerm schema & glossary dictionary structure","description":"# JargonTerm Schema Design

## Goal
Define the TypeScript interface and data structure for glossary terms that balances comprehensiveness with usability.

## Schema (adapted from reference implementation)

```typescript
export interface JargonTerm {
  /** The display term (e.g., "C. elegans") */
  term: string;
  
  /** One-line definition for quick tooltips (~100 chars) */
  short: string;
  
  /** Full explanation in plain English (2-4 sentences) */
  long: string;
  
  /** "Think of it like..." analogy for non-experts */
  analogy?: string;
  
  /** Why this term matters in the Brenner context */
  why?: string;
  
  /** Related term keys for discovery */
  related?: string[];
  
  /** Category for filtering */
  category: JargonCategory;
}

type JargonCategory = 
  | "operators"      // Brenner operators (⊘, 𝓛, etc.)
  | "brenner"        // Core Brenner concepts
  | "biology"        // Scientific/biology terms
  | "bayesian"       // Bayesian/statistical terms
  | "method"         // Scientific method terms
  | "project";       // BrennerBot-specific terms
```

## Dictionary Structure
- Keys: lowercase with hyphens (e.g., "c-elegans", "level-split")
- File: `apps/web/src/lib/jargon.ts`
- Export: `jargonDictionary: Record<string, JargonTerm>`
- Helper: `getJargon(termKey: string): JargonTerm | undefined`

## Key Decisions
1. Use `short` for tooltips, `long` for bottom sheet/glossary
2. `analogy` optional but strongly encouraged for complex terms
3. `why` connects term to Brenner methodology specifically
4. `related` enables discovery but should be curated (2-4 terms max)
5. Categories enable filtering without rigid hierarchy

## Deliverable
- `apps/web/src/lib/jargon.ts` with types and empty dictionary
- `getJargon()` helper function
- Export types for use in component/page

## Testing
- Type safety via TypeScript
- No runtime tests needed for schema (schema is verified by usage)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T00:57:07.45461325-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T01:01:39.595120196-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T01:01:39.595120196-05:00","close_reason":"JargonTerm schema implemented in apps/web/src/lib/jargon.ts with 14+ operator terms, 6 core Brenner concepts, biology terms, and getJargon() helper.","labels":["design","glossary"]}
